sanskrit:
तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्।
भवामि न चिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्॥
transliteration: teṣām ahaṃ samuddhartā mṛtyu-saṃsāra-sāgarāt | bhavāmi na cirāt pārtha mayy āveśita-cetasām ||
literal_meaning: For them I become the swift deliverer from the ocean of death-and-rebirth, O Pārtha, whose minds are absorbed in Me.
contextual_meaning: When a person fully delegates every action to the Divine and keeps attention single-pointedly on that relationship, the nervous system stops treating life as a threat; existential dread dissolves because the Self is no longer identified with the impermanent body-mind.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to an internalized benevolent presence; ego boundaries soften without dissolving into pathology.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver finally asks for help and discovers that delegating tasks to a trusted team lowers cortisol and restores sleep within days.
anchor_text: Surrender fully to the Divine and be freed from the cycle of birth and death.
